logistics.lodging_strategy: The event takes place in the Trentino region with access to Madonna di Campiglio and surrounding areas.

final_verdict.one_liner: A mid-distance Dolomite gran fondo with strong local heritage and scenic appeal, ideal for riders seeking authentic Italian cycling culture.

videos.title: 2022 TRENTO UCI GRANFONDO WORLD CHAMPIONSHIPS RACE COURSE PREVIEW
videos.description: Discover the beautiful race course of 2022 UCI Granfondo World Championships in Trento (Italy). The 143 km long course features the iconic Monte Bondone from 2 different sides and a flat finish after 3900 m of elevation gain. Listen to Andrea's route description and preview all the climbs and descends. One More Ride organize the fully supported packages for the event, offering 5-8-10 or even 12 days tours. Check all the options on https://www.one-more-ride.com/uci-granfondo-world-champs-vip-pack

videos.title: Gran Fondo Survival Guide
videos.description:
Four tips on how to make mass start events, like Gran Fondos and Sportives a memorable and enjoyable event.

Special thanks to my friend Pembroke Kenner for providing some coverage of Fondo Riding at the METRO ATLANTA CYCLING CLUB One Love Century.
https://youtu.be/fuTqkET7Gko

videos.title: Whistler Gran Fondo 2023
videos.description:
GoPro recording of the 2023 Whistler Gran Fondo (122km). Official time: 3:54:22
---
0:35 Rolling through Stanley Park
9:05 Lions Gate Bridge
12:55 Taylor Way climb
16:20 Highway 1 to Horseshoe Bay
35:15 Horseshoe Bay / Rest Stop #1
51:05 Lions Bay
54:25 Descent to Brunswick Beach
01:10:25 Porteau Cove / Rest Stop #2
01:18:10 Furry Creek Climb
01:24:25 Descent to Britannia Beach
01:37:40 Descent to Squamish
01:43:30 Stawamus Chief
01:46:45 Dodging cones through Squamish

02:10:45 Rest Stop #3
02:21:05 Cheakamus climb

02:43:45 Rest Stop #4
03:00:40 Daisy Lake
03:19:45 Brandywine Falls / Rest Stop #5
03:44:40 Arriving to Whistler area
03:53:40 Whistler Village
03:54:35 Finish line

videos.title: Strade Bianche Gran Fondo: Cycling Perfection !
videos.description:
Last weekend I travelled to Siena, in Tuscany, Italy to be "on tour" with my good friends at Garda Bike Hotel, who had laid on some spectacular cycling and events over the course of the Strade Bianche Classics race - cumulating in me taking part in the Gran Fondo which follow's the lady's and men's race on the Saturday.  

It was my first time cycling in Tuscany, and wow!!! Simply wow!! Beautiful undulating countryside, with contrasting silky tarmac and plenty of iconic white gravel.  It was an exceptional event, within an exceptional weekend with exceptional company.

You'll see extracts of the UCI pro men's race plus my own effort navigating the Gran Fondo, Strade Bianche, including the iconic white gravel sectors and the famous final climb into Siena.  An experience I will cherish forever.  

Many thanks to Nicola, Davide, Alberto, Stefano and Daniel from Garda Bike Hotel - always consummate hosts, and always a ton of fun!! 

https://gardabikehotel.com

To translate the subtitles to your preferred language, please go to the settings cog on the video, and make sure subtitles are turned on.  Then select auto-translate, and then select the language you prefer.

Please leave those comments and hit that subscribe 

Thanks ever so much for all of your support! And whoever you are, what ever your do, please remember to LIVE THRIVE AND STAY HEALTHY ! 

Get Ignite by Fred V & Grafix, Amy J Pryce here https://lickd.lnk.to/ST7xB9ID!Bike%20Racing%20Without%20Mercy License ID: a4pjKM73x8E Get this and other songs for your next YouTube video at https://lickd.co

Follow my journey  
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bikeracingwithoutmercy/
Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/BRWM

quotes.text: This is really similar to a Giro d'Italia mountain stage - 143 kilometers with 4000 meters of elevation and three major climbs including Monte Bondone two times. The first ascent up to Monte Bondone is very long, 20 kilometers from 200 meters up to 1550 meters, with an average of 6.5-6.7% but the last section gets steep again with gradients that never go below 9-10%.

We have a long descent coming - 20 kilometers down on the other side of the valley, but remember we have to come up Monte Bondone another time from a different side. This is definitely a key part of the race with maximum speeds hitting high kilometers per hour on the great surface and beautiful road.

key_challenges.name: Sector One Start
key_challenges.description: The start is seriously mad with people going ballistic, like the start of a max effort crit race on Zwift. Multiple sizeable crashes occur early on.

key_challenges.name: Sector Three Radde
key_challenges.description: The first big climb of the day where you quickly discover it's much harder to climb on gravel. Features forgiving gradients but still challenging terrain.

key_challenges.name: Monte Bondone First Ascent
key_challenges.description: Very long 20 kilometer climb from 200m to 1550m elevation (1300m gain), averaging 6.5-6.7%. Divided into three sections: first 10k pretty steep, easy middle section at Castiga for rest and hydration, then steep again with gradients never going below 9-10%.

key_challenges.name: Monte Bondone Second Ascent
key_challenges.description: Climb from Sopramonte to real top at 1650m, with 1200m elevation gain over 16k (7-7.5% average). Never less than 7% with some 8-9% sections. Long and challenging with beautiful road and lots of shade.

terrain_notes.text: Sector two provides six kilometers of gently undulating and pleasingly fast pristine Tuscan gravel where 200 watts makes you feel like you're going at light speed.
The white gravel sectors require a lot of concentration, and it's much harder to climb on gravel than expected.
Beautiful paved roads with great surface for descending. Long descent of 20k on other side of valley - not technical at all, super fast with few corners and some u-turns but nothing really difficult.

gear_mentions.text: The Pinarello gravel with 52 millimeter tyres truly soaks up the lumps and bumps, enabling good progress at 240 to 270 watts.
Make sure your bike is tuned and ready with the right type of gearing for the type of course you're going to be riding.

race_day_tips.text: Hold the line at the crazy start, let them come through and find yourself a nice civilized group riding at nice tempo rather than getting caught up in the ballistic opening pace.
During the race, especially on hot days, use the easy middle section at Castiga on the first climb to rest and get hydrated - this will be very important.
Better to be in a small group during the rolling section between climbs to eat and drink effectively.
Plan for weather changes with kit to deal with 10-15 degree temperature variations and unexpected rain.

additional_quotes.text: If you're planning to travel with your bike, make sure you're comfortable with assembly and disassembly. The alternative is renting from big concept stores like Specialized, Trek, or Giant - reserve online with a credit card so you have accountability.
My experience with mass start events is very diverse - I've been involved in route planning, route design, logistical planning, sag structure, event support, and even pacing groups at events.

rider_intel.search_text: The Granfondo Moser features multiple challenging climbs including the iconic Monte Bondone ascended twice. The first ascent covers 20 kilometers with 1300m elevation gain, averaging 6.5-6.7% with three distinct sections including steep opening and closing portions separated by an easier middle section at Castiga ideal for hydration. The second ascent from Sopramonte to the 1650m summit covers 1200m over 16k at 7-7.5% average, never dropping below 7% gradient. Riders describe beautiful paved roads with fast, non-technical descents spanning 20+ kilometers. The event starts with an extremely aggressive pace described as ballistic with multiple early crashes. Gravel sections require significant concentration and are notably harder to climb than expected. The course features pristine Tuscan white gravel sectors where moderate power feels exceptionally fast. Weather can vary significantly requiring preparation for 10-15 degree temperature swings. Strategic pacing and group positioning are crucial for managing the demanding elevation profile effectively.
